title: iPod touch
body: ![](http://images.apple.com/euro/ipodtouch/gallery/images/ipodtouch_image4_20080909.jpg) I have read this [post about the iPhone](http://enriqueaguilera.com/2008/11/22/iphone-3g-recomendaciones-despues-de-4-meses/), many things can be extrapolated to the iPod touch, it is practically like an iPhone but narrower and without a phone or camera or GPS, but I am more than happy with it, with its interface and its usability. Despite my profession, I have never been a fan of Apple, they have always been ahead but they have always been somewhat limited, it is true that this limitation greatly facilitates usability, in terms of gadget design it is the best, very beautiful. Until I got an iPod touch in a giveaway and I was hooked. And like any good Apple product, both the iPhone and the iPod touch have their limitations, for example there is no option to select, copy and paste. It is also mandatory to connect it to the computer through iTunes (for which it is necessary to register and leave your credit card), and that is a problem if you want to upload/download files from another device or on Linux systems (there is software that recognizes iPod models but not the touch/iPhone). The camera is one of the things that I find missing from the iPod touch, also the Bluetooth or a microphone to be able to record or use it as an IP phone (Skype) and the GPS would not be bad at all in this model, although I am amazed that without it it will locate you the same if you have Wifi coverage. There are very good applications for the iPod touch and iPhone, many free or very cheap (1-4 euros). If you have a computer that you use as a media center, connected to your music system, I recommend Remote, a software that allows you to manage iTunes on your computer remotely. I also have a VNC client, to see your computer's desktop on the iPhone/iPod touch, or specific applications for Twitter or Facebook.
